latest.title: Tawny frogmouths don't move like owls
latest.script_key: this bird stays dead still not because it s asleep but because it s hunting watch the beak no flutter no flicker just a slow pivot when prey moves that s why it looks like bark even in plain light did you think all night birds hunt like owls

latest.title: Sharks sense tiny electric fields
latest.script_key: sharks can feel electricity in the water watch the head before the turn because sharks have sensory pores that detect tiny electric fields from other animals that helps them hunt when sight is limited the ocean is full of signals we cannot feel would you swim differently

latest.title: Forests make cooler air under the canopy
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Peaks that aren't as old as they look
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you those jagged snow covered peaks weren t always there ice and wind carve away at rock faster than plates push up that s why some alpine ridges look ancient but are geologically toddlers watch how the sharp edges soften like butter under a hot knife would you climb one that s still growing

latest.title: snow peaks hide a flood risk you don't see
latest.script_key: these peaks keep water locked up see how the white patches aren t just snow they re glaciers that s why they release meltwater slow and steady but here s what blew my mind if they vanish cities downstream lose their slow faucet what would your town do without it

latest.title: Rivers carve bends by stealing from one bank
latest.script_key: rivers reveal the river bend first watch closely because faster water cuts that bank while slower water drops sediment inside the curve over time the whole river shifts across the landscape a bend is motion written in mud which water shape next

latest.title: Squirrels use lake trees for more than shade
latest.script_key: this squirrel isn t just snacking it s digging into the bark to drink the sap leaking out that s its emergency water sips quietly see how it leans in roots pull water up from the lake below so the tree s always juicy that s why squirrels camp near lakes even when food s scarce

latest.title: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
latest.script_key: plants use trap hairs to count touches before closing watch the trap hairs because the plant waits for more than one touch before spending energy on a snap that delay helps it ignore rain and debris then close when prey is real which plant trick should we decode next

latest.title: Birds pollinate flowers with their feet
latest.script_key: birds pollinate with their feet watch this warbler its toes are covered in yellow dust that s pollen stuck to it from the last flower because birds don t just sip nectar they carry it did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Ducks carry waterproof oil on their feathers
latest.script_key: line up ducks don t care watch their eyes one always checks behind that s why they walk in a row the leader breaks water resistance the rest save energy i thought they just liked order now i know it s physics on webbed feet

latest.title: Hawks see movement you can't
